title: Universal cable tray mounting support spacer
abstract: The present invention is directed to a universal tray mount and support spacer particularly used for supporting and installing electrical cable and conduit trays and carriers, which is constituted by a stackable columnar shaped member which is able to be located and attached into openings provided in the flat surface of trays, the support spacer has a hole through which fixing means are inserted and secured to the level or uneven mounting surfaces and to flat roofs by way of an additional foot fitting. The support spacer may also be stacked to lengthen it and thus allowing the cable tray to be secured at a greater distance from the wall or floor surface.
claims:
Claims Claims 1) A universal tray mounting support spacer particularly for electrical cable and conduit trays and carriers, constituted by: A stackable columnar shaped spacer which is intended to be located and attached into openings provided in the planar surfaces of the tray or carrier, characterized in that the support spacer has flexing prongs formed from its body which have grapnel teeth to their extremity, which hold the support spacer in place when they are passed into the hole provided in the cable tray planar surface, the mounting spacer provides a means by which a screw or bolt may be then used to secure the spacer and tray to a wall, floor or ceiling surface on installation, the spacer may be stacked to enable trays to be spaced at greater distance from the wall, floor or ceiling fixture surface, a secondary wider foot may be attached to the support spacer to aid fixture to particular surfaces. 2) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed in claim 1 wherein, flexible prongs may be compressed when hand held and inserted, to allow their passage through openings in the surface of electrical cable trays or carriers. 3) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed in claim 2 wherein, compressed prongs located to the extremity of the support spacer cylindric body, hook onto the opposing face of the cable tray surface when they have passed through the opening provided and when released do return to their primary position to bear onto the inner edges of the hole forming a tight hold. 4) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed in claim 3 wherein, the angle of the return in the prong secures the prong into the perforation enhancing the grip provided by the return of the prong to its initial position once released. 5) A universal support spacer as claimed in claim 4 wherein, the angle of the return allows for different thicknesses of tray to be gripped. 6) A universal support spacer as claimed in claim 5 wherein, the spacing of the prongs enables use of the support spacer with trays and their manufacturers on a universal basis. 

 7) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed in claim 6 wherein, prongs which hold the support spacer in the openings found on a cable tray, may not pass back through the opening once in place, due to the prongs providing purchase, aided by toothed portions on the prongs extending slightly beyond the openings diameter. 8) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed wherein, at least two or more support spacers may be attached to each other in a progressing columnar length, means provided by corresponding fittings to the upper and lower portions of all spacers. 9) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed in claim 8 wherein, a cable tray for electrical or conduit cables, may be held at various heights from the surface to which it is mounted. 10) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed wherein, spacer attached to a cable tray may be offered up to a mounting surface for direct drilling of holes through their centre with or without prior positional marking. 1 ) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed wherein, a more capacious foot member may be attached to the bottom of the support spacer by means provided. 12) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed in claim 11 wherein, a more capacious foot member provides an option for installation on flat roofs where a cable tray typically serves plant and equipment mounted on a flat roof sometimes with delicate membrane coverings, supports cannot be fixed to the structure therefore a broad based support is utilised to spread the load and protect the integrity of the roof 13) A universal mounting support spacer as claimed uses a narrowed columnar shape which enables more successful installation to uneven surfaces.
